note: District names and descriptions based on publicly available Loudoun County zoning ordinance information. Verify all details against the current adopted ordinance at https://library.municode.com/va/loudoun_county/codes/codified_ordinances

PD-IP.district_description: Planned development district for high-quality industrial, office, technology, and compatible uses developed under an approved development plan. This is the primary zoning district for data center development in the Ashburn and Sterling portions of Loudoun County. Development standards are governed by both the base ordinance and approved proffer conditions specific to each development plan.
PD-IP.ordinance_chapter: See current Loudoun County Zoning Ordinance
PD-IP.ordinance_section: PD-IP district regulations — verify current section with https://library.municode.com/va/loudoun_county
PD-IP.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility use authorized in PD-IP with an approved development plan. Most data centers in Loudoun operate in PD-IP under specific proffered conditions. DCOD overlay may apply.
PD-IP.reviewer_notes: Loudoun County's PD-IP zoning is the foundation of Data Center Alley. Every major colocation and hyperscale campus in the Ashburn/Sterling area is built on PD-IP-zoned land. Development plans and proffers are the primary regulatory instruments — they often include site-specific setbacks, noise limits, design standards, and phasing requirements that supersede base ordinance minimums.

I1.district_description: Light industrial district for warehouse, light manufacturing, office, research and development, and compatible uses with minimal external effects. May permit data center use with special approval.
I1.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility may require a Special Use Permit (SUP) or equivalent approval in I1. Verify with Loudoun County Planning.

I2.district_description: Heavy industrial district for uses with more significant operational characteristics including noise, traffic, and site coverage. More permissive than I1 for by-right uses.
I2.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility generally permitted by right in I2. Verify with Loudoun County Planning.

AR1.district_name: Agricultural Rural — 1 dwelling unit per 30 acres
AR1.district_description: Rural agricultural district covering most of western Loudoun County. Very low density residential use permitted. Designed to protect farmland, rural character, open space, and natural resources. Data center and industrial uses are not permitted uses in this district.
AR1.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility is not a permitted or similar use in AR1. Rezoning would be required.
AR1.reviewer_notes: AR1 covers the majority of western Loudoun County. The Loudoun County Comprehensive Plan generally directs development to the eastern portion of the county (the Revised Urban Policy Area and the Transition Policy Area), away from AR1-zoned rural areas.

JLMA-3.district_name: Joint Land Management Area — 3 dwelling units per acre
JLMA-3.district_description: Residential transition zone in the Joint Land Management Area, shared between Loudoun County and adjacent municipalities. Permits residential development at up to 3 dwelling units per acre. Commercial and industrial uses are not permitted.
JLMA-3.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility is not permitted in JLMA-3. This is a residential district.

B2.district_description: General commercial district for retail, office, service, and community-serving uses. Not intended for industrial or data center uses.
B2.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility is not a standard permitted or conditional use in B2. Would require rezoning to an appropriate industrial or planned development district.

PD-OP.district_description: Planned development office park district. Primarily office and professional service uses. Technology-oriented development may be permitted with an approved development plan.
PD-OP.dc_eligibility_summary: Data Center Facility eligibility in PD-OP depends on the specific approved development plan and whether it includes data center as a permitted use. Verify with Loudoun County Planning.
